title: Wiring the WordPress adapter
summary: How the adapter talks to wp/v2/{posts,pages,categories,tags,users} and turns each row into one ACT node.

content.text: The adapter's enumerate step pulls every collection you opt into via config.include and yields raw WordPress rows. transform then maps each row to one ACT node: titles become title, the rendered HTML becomes a prose block, and the rendered excerpt becomes the node's summary.
Categories and tags are emitted as branch nodes so the tree has structure — posts hang off their primary category. Pages keep their parent relationship intact, mirroring the WordPress page hierarchy.
